Bellis perennis, or the daisy, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Bellis perennis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with daisy include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
